paper_title: Offshore CHIRP and Marine Terrace Methods

methods.description: A marine seismic survey was conducted to locate the Carrizal and related faults offshore using the Scripps submeter-resolution Edgetech Subscan CHIRP system.

steps.description: Conducted a marine seismic survey aboard a Mexican Navy vessel in August 2008, using the Scripps submeter-resolution Edgetech Subscan CHIRP system.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct marine seismic survey using Edgetech Subscan CHIRP system

steps.description: The CHIRP profiler was operated with a 50 ms swept pulse of 0.7-3 kHz and provided decimeter vertical resolution and sub-bottom penetration >50 m.
steps.searchable_summary: operate CHIRP profiler with specified pulse and resolution

steps.description: All data were digitally recorded in an internal EdgeTech JSF format and later converted to SEG-Y format with real-time GPS navigation.
steps.searchable_summary: record and convert seismic data to SEG-Y format with GPS navigation

steps.description: Data were processed using SIOSEIS and imported to Kingdom Suite and IVS Fledermaus software packages for interpretation.
steps.searchable_summary: process and interpret seismic data using SIOSEIS, Kingdom Suite, and IVS Fledermaus

methods.description: Analysis of uplifted marine terraces to understand the rates of the offshore Carrizal faulting.

steps.description: Studied a series of uplifted marine terraces well exposed along the western side of La Paz Bay.
steps.searchable_summary: study uplifted marine terraces in La Paz Bay

steps.description: Mapped and described the terraces, and measured the height of the terraces above sea level.
Estimated ages of Quaternary marine terraces by analyzing the extent of racemization in amino acids preserved within carbonate fossils.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze amino acid racemization to estimate terrace ages

steps.description: Corroborated amino acid racemization ratios by comparison to U-Th ages reported for marine terraces in the northern part of the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: validate racemization ratios with U-Th ages

steps.description: Used terrace ages with the terrace heights to determine surface uplift rates and inferred offset.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate surface uplift rates using terrace ages and heights

methods.description: Analysis of amino acid racemization in carbonate fossils to estimate the ages of marine terraces.

steps.description: Collected samples from similar, contiguous deposits with depths >1 m to minimize the effect of seasonal and diurnal temperature fluctuations.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from deposits deeper than 1 m

steps.description: Prepared samples according to the procedures of Kaufman and Manley (1998) and Wehmiller and Miller (2000) for reverse-phase and ion-exchange, high-performance liquid chromatography analysis.
Processed samples in the Northern Arizona University Amino Acid Geochronology Laboratory.
